data.title: Bakery Stabilizers Market to Reach USD 2.07 Million by 2036 | Europe & North America Demand Surge with Top Companies Tate & Lyle, Palsgaard
data.description:
Bakery Stabilizers Market is set to grow steadily, driven by demand for improved texture, shelf life, and clean-label ingredients in baked goods worldwide.

NEWARK, DE / ACCESS Newswire / April 1, 2026 / The global Bakery Stabilizers Market is expanding steadily as food manufacturers prioritize texture consistency, shelf-life extension, and clean-label formulations in baked products. Valued at USD 1.36 Million in 2026 , the market is projected to reach USD 2.07 Million by 2036 , growing at a CAGR of 4.3% over the forecast period.

According to Future Market Insights (FMI) , demand is being sustained by the convergence of industrial bakery automation, evolving consumer expectations, and regulatory pressure for transparent ingredient labeling .

Bakery Stabilizers Market Metrics (2026-2036)

Market Indicator

Value

Market Size (2026)

USD 1.36 Million

Market Value (2036)

USD 2.07 Million

Global CAGR

4.3%

Leading Stabilizer Type

Emulsifiers (48% Share)

Leading Application

Food Manufacturing (28% Share)

Fastest-Growing Region

China (5.8% CAGR)

Discover Growth Opportunities in the Market - Get Your Sample Report Now: https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/sample/rep-gb-27199

Market Overview: Functional Ingredients Powering Modern Baking

Bakery stabilizers are functional food ingredients used to:

  • Improve dough structure and elasticity

  • Enhance crumb softness and texture

  • Retain moisture and extend shelf life

  • Ensure consistency in large-scale production

They include:

  • Emulsifiers (dough strengthening + softening)

  • Hydrocolloids (moisture retention, viscosity control)

  • Enzymes (process optimization, shelf-life extension)

  • Blended systems (multi-functional performance)

These ingredients are essential for industrial bakeries, commercial production lines, and packaged food supply chains .

Key Growth Drivers: Industrialization and Clean-Label Shift

Industrial Bakery Expansion

The transition from artisan to industrial baking is accelerating in emerging markets such as China, India, and Brazil.

Drivers include:

  • Urbanization and rising packaged food demand

  • Growth of modern retail and distribution networks

  • Need for scalable, automated production systems

Stabilizers play a critical role in maintaining product consistency across high-speed production lines .

Clean-Label Reformulation

Consumers increasingly demand:

  • Shorter ingredient lists

  • Natural and recognizable components

  • Transparency in food labeling

This is pushing manufacturers to:

  • Reformulate stabilizers with natural alternatives

  • Reduce synthetic additives

  • Maintain performance without compromising label simplicity

Shelf-Life Optimization

Extended shelf life is crucial for:

  • Packaged bakery products

  • Export and long-distance distribution

  • E-commerce and modern retail supply chains

Stabilizers help prevent:

  • Staling

  • Moisture loss

  • Texture degradation

Segment Insights: Emulsifiers and Industrial Applications Dominate

By Stabilizer Type: Emulsifiers (48% Share)

Emulsifiers lead the market due to their dual functionality :

  • Strengthening dough structure

  • Softening crumb texture

Their efficiency in single-step integration makes them ideal for industrial use.

By Application: Food Manufacturing (28% Share)

Food manufacturing dominates due to:

  • Large-scale production requirements

  • Need for consistent quality and texture

  • Integration with automated systems

High-Growth Segment: Artisan Bakery

Artisan bakeries are emerging as a fast-growing segment due to:

  • Premium product positioning

  • Demand for consistent quality

  • Adoption of modern stabilizer systems

Regional Outlook: Asia Leads Volume Growth, West Drives Innovation

China (5.8% CAGR)

China leads global growth, driven by:

  • Rapid industrial bakery expansion

  • Government support for food manufacturing

  • Increasing urban consumption

India (5.4% CAGR)

India's market is supported by:

  • Growth in packaged food demand

  • Expansion of bakery chains

  • Cost-effective stabilizer solutions

Germany (4.9% CAGR)

Germany focuses on:

  • High-quality, premium formulations

  • Strict EU regulatory compliance

  • Advanced food processing technologies

United States (4.1% CAGR)

The U.S. market benefits from:

  • Mature bakery industry

  • Strong clean-label trend

  • Innovation in functional ingredients

Japan (3.2% CAGR)

Japan represents a mature market with demand driven by:

  • Precision formulations

  • Premium bakery products

  • Quality-focused consumers

Market Dynamics: Functionality Meets Regulation

Key Drivers

  • Rising demand for processed and packaged bakery products

  • Clean-label and natural ingredient trends

  • Growth in industrial bakery automation

Key Challenges

  • Raw material price volatility

  • Competition from alternative ingredients (enzymes, proteins)

  • Regulatory compliance across regions

Key Trends

  • Integration of smart production systems (IoT, automated dosing)

  • Development of customized stabilizer blends

  • Increasing role of digital quality monitoring and traceability

Competitive Landscape: Innovation and Technical Support Define Leadership

The bakery stabilizers market is moderately consolidated, with leading companies competing on:

  • Product performance

  • Technical support and formulation expertise

  • Regulatory compliance and validation services

Key Players Include:

  • CARAGUM International

  • Cargill, Incorporated

  • Tate & Lyle PLC

  • Ingredion Incorporated

  • Vantage Specialty Chemicals

  • Advanced Food Systems, Inc.

  • Palsgaard A/S

  • IFF

These companies focus on:

  • Clean-label stabilizer development

  • Customized solutions for specific applications

  • Integration with industrial production systems

Strategic Outlook: From Ingredients to Integrated Food Solutions

The bakery stabilizers market is evolving from basic ingredient supply to integrated food technology solutions .

Future growth will be driven by:

  • Clean-label innovation without compromising performance

  • Application-specific formulations for diverse bakery products

  • Digital integration in production processes

Manufacturers that combine functional performance, regulatory compliance, and technical support will gain a competitive advantage in this evolving market.

For an in-depth analysis of these trends and to access the complete Bakery Stabilizers Market Forecast through 2036, visit: https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/reports/bakery-stabilizers-market

data.title: Best Equipment Financing Companies USA 2026 Rankings Released
data.description:
Access to reliable equipment financing is essential for businesses looking to grow, expand operations, or upgrade outdated machinery. Whether it's medical devices, construction equipment, or manufacturing tools, financing allows companies to preserve working capital while investing in productivity.

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / April 1, 2026 / IRAEmpire is pleased to present its latest guide to the Best Equipment Financing Companies in the USA, based on accessibility, funding speed, flexibility, transparency, and customer satisfaction.

After evaluating leading providers, the following companies stand out:

  • Credion Finance - #1 Best Overall Equipment Financing Company

  • Credibly - #2 Best for Diverse Options

  • National Business Capital - #3 Best Customer Support

These lenders consistently deliver strong value across different industries and business sizes.

According to Michael Hunt, Senior Writer at IRAEmpire, "Equipment financing has become a critical growth tool for businesses in 2026. The right lender doesn't just provide capital-they provide flexibility and speed when it matters most."

View the Full Best Equipment Financing Companies List Here

Best Equipment Financing Companies in the USA - Official Rankings

#1 - Credion Finance (Best Overall Equipment Financing Company)

Credion Finance takes the top spot due to its strong combination of flexibility, accessibility, and business-focused solutions.

Read Credion Finance Reviews and Analysis Here

Why Credion Finance Is #1

  • High approval rates across industries

  • Flexible financing structures

  • Fast application and approval process

  • Strong focus on small and mid-sized businesses

Credion Finance works with a wide range of borrowers, including startups and growing companies, making it a highly accessible option.

Key Features

  • Competitive interest rates

  • Quick funding timelines

  • Customized repayment structures

  • Companies needing flexible financing

  • Businesses seeking fast approvals

#2 - Credibly (Best for Fast Funding)

Credibly is known for its speed and efficiency, making it a strong choice for businesses that need quick access to capital.

Read Credibly Reviews and Analysis Here

Why Credibly Ranks #2

  • Rapid approval and funding process

  • Streamlined application

  • Strong reputation in alternative lending

Key Benefits

  • Fast turnaround times

  • Flexible qualification criteria

  • Multiple financing options

Ideal For

  • Businesses needing urgent equipment financing

  • Companies with limited credit history

  • Small businesses looking for quick capital

#3 - National Business Capital (Best for Flexible Financing Solutions)

National Business Capital stands out for offering a wide range of financing products and tailored solutions.

Read National Business Capital Reviews and Analysis Here

Why National Business Capital Is #3

  • Extensive lender network

  • Customized financing options

  • Ability to handle larger funding amounts

Highlights

  • Access to multiple lenders

  • Competitive terms for qualified borrowers

  • Strong support team

Best For

  • Businesses seeking larger financing amounts

  • Companies needing tailored solutions

  • Established businesses with strong financials

How IRAEmpire Ranked the Best Equipment Financing Companies

To determine the top providers, IRAEmpire evaluated companies based on:

1. Accessibility and Approval Rates

Ease of qualification for different business types

2. Speed of Funding

Application processing and funding timelines

3. Financing Flexibility

Loan structures, leasing options, and repayment terms

4. Transparency

Clear terms, fees, and pricing

5. Customer Experience

Support quality and borrower satisfaction

What Is Equipment Financing?

Equipment financing is a type of business funding used to purchase machinery, tools, vehicles, or technology needed for day-to-day operations. The equipment itself often serves as collateral, which reduces risk for lenders and makes it easier for businesses-especially small and mid-sized ones-to qualify compared to unsecured loans.

Learn How to Get Equipment Financing Here

This type of financing can be structured as:

  • Equipment loans (ownership at the end)

  • Equipment leases (lower upfront cost, flexible terms)

With an equipment loan, the business borrows money to purchase the equipment and makes fixed monthly payments over a set period. Once the loan is fully repaid, the business owns the asset outright. This is ideal for equipment with a long useful life.

In contrast, equipment leasing allows businesses to use the equipment without immediately owning it. Leasing typically requires lower upfront costs and may include options to upgrade, renew, or purchase the equipment at the end of the term. This is especially useful for industries where technology evolves quickly.

Equipment financing is widely used across industries such as construction, healthcare, manufacturing, transportation, and food services. It enables businesses to acquire essential tools without tying up large amounts of capital.

Additionally, financing can help improve cash flow, as businesses can spread the cost over time rather than making a large upfront investment. In some cases, there may also be tax advantages, such as deducting lease payments or depreciation, depending on the structure and local regulations.

Overall, equipment financing is a practical and scalable solution for businesses looking to grow efficiently while maintaining financial flexibility.

Key Benefits of Equipment Financing

Equipment financing offers several strategic advantages for businesses looking to grow without straining their cash reserves.

Learn About the Best Equipment Financing Providers Here

Preserve Working Capital
One of the biggest benefits is the ability to conserve cash. Instead of making a large upfront investment, businesses can spread the cost over time through manageable monthly payments. This allows them to maintain liquidity for other critical expenses such as payroll, marketing, and inventory.

Access to the Latest Technology
Financing enables businesses to acquire modern, high-quality equipment that may otherwise be too expensive. Staying up to date with the latest technology can improve competitiveness, enhance product quality, and increase overall productivity. This is especially important in industries where outdated equipment can quickly become a disadvantage.

Improve Operational Efficiency
Upgrading or acquiring the right equipment can streamline operations, reduce downtime, and improve output. Efficient machinery often leads to faster production, better service delivery, and reduced maintenance costs over time.

Flexible Financing Structures
Many lenders offer customizable repayment plans, including seasonal payments or deferred structures. This flexibility allows businesses to align payments with their cash flow cycles.

Potential Tax Advantages
Depending on how the financing is structured, businesses may be able to deduct interest payments, lease expenses, or depreciation. These tax benefits can reduce the overall cost of acquiring equipment.

Who Should Consider Equipment Financing?

Equipment financing is suitable for a wide range of industries and business types, particularly those that rely heavily on physical assets to generate revenue.

Construction Companies
Heavy machinery such as excavators, loaders, and cranes often require significant investment. Financing allows contractors to take on larger projects without tying up capital.

Medical Practices
Healthcare providers frequently need advanced diagnostic and treatment equipment. Financing makes it easier to adopt new technologies that improve patient care and expand services.

Manufacturing Businesses
Manufacturers rely on machinery to maintain production efficiency. Financing helps them scale operations and replace outdated equipment without disrupting cash flow.

Transportation and Logistics Firms
Trucks, delivery vehicles, and fleet upgrades are essential for logistics businesses. Financing supports expansion and operational reliability.

Restaurants and Retail Businesses
From kitchen appliances to point-of-sale systems, equipment plays a vital role in daily operations. Financing allows businesses to upgrade without large upfront costs.

Ultimately, any business that depends on equipment to operate, scale, or improve efficiency can benefit from financing solutions.

How to Choose the Right Equipment Financing Company

When selecting an equipment financing provider, it's important to look beyond just approval and focus on long-term value, flexibility, and reliability.

Interest Rates and Total Cost
Start by evaluating not just the interest rate, but the total cost of financing. This includes fees, documentation charges, and any prepayment penalties. A lower monthly payment may seem attractive, but it could mean paying more over time.

Approval Requirements
Different lenders have different qualification criteria. Some require strong credit scores and established revenue, while others specialize in working with newer or lower-credit businesses. Choose a provider whose requirements align with your financial profile to increase your chances of approval.

Funding Speed
If your business needs equipment urgently, funding speed becomes critical. Some lenders can approve and disburse funds within 24-72 hours, while traditional lenders may take weeks. Faster funding can help you avoid operational delays.

Flexibility of Repayment Terms
Look for lenders that offer flexible repayment structures, such as seasonal payments, step-up plans, or deferred payments. This is especially important for businesses with fluctuating cash flow.

Reputation and Customer Reviews
Research the lender's reputation through verified reviews, testimonials, and third-party platforms. Consistent positive feedback and strong customer support are good indicators of reliability.

In addition to these factors, consider whether the lender offers both leasing and loan options, as this can give you more flexibility in structuring your financing. Also, evaluate how transparent they are during the process-clear communication and written terms are essential.

Finally, avoid lenders with unclear pricing, aggressive sales tactics, or hidden fees. Choosing the right financing partner can make a significant difference in your business's ability to grow efficiently and sustainably.

View the Top Equipment Financing Providers in USA

Final Thoughts

Equipment financing continues to play a vital role in business growth across the United States. Choosing the right provider can help businesses scale efficiently without straining cash flow.

About IRAEmpire

IRAEmpire.com provides independent research, rankings, and educational content across business finance, investment strategies, and lending solutions. Its mission is to help businesses make informed decisions through transparent, data-driven insights.

CONTACT:
Ryan Paulson
ryan@iraempire.com

SOURCE: IRAEmpire LLC

data.title: PINS Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Pinterest, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
data.description:
PINS Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Pinterest, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm

PINS Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Pinterest, Inc. Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm

PR Newswire

LOS ANGELES, April 1, 2026

LOS ANGELES, April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Schall Law Firm, a national shareholder rights litigation firm, reminds investors of a class action lawsuit against Pinterest, Inc. ("Pinterest" or "the Company") (NYSE: PINS) for violations of §§10(b) and 20(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and Rule 10b-5 promulgated thereunder by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Investors who purchased the Company's securities between February 7, 2025 and February 12, 2026, inclusive (the "Class Period"), are encouraged to contact the firm before May 29, 2026.

If you are a shareholder who suffered a loss, click here to participate.

We also encourage you to contact Brian Schall of the Schall Law Firm, 2049 Century Park East, Suite 2460, Los Angeles, CA 90067, at 310-301-3335, to discuss your rights free of charge. You can also reach us through the firm's website at www.schallfirm.com, or by email at bschall@schallfirm.com.

The class, in this case, has not yet been certified, and until certification occurs, you are not represented by an attorney. If you choose to take no action, you can remain an absent class member.

According to the Complaint, the Company made false and misleading statements to the market. Pinterest suffered from reduced advertising revenue from partners. The Company downplayed the impact of tariffs on its business, including their impact on advertising partners. Based on these facts, the Company's public statements were false and materially misleading throughout the class period. When the market learned the truth about Pinterest, investors suffered damages.

Join the case to recover your losses

The Schall Law Firm represents investors around the world and specializes in securities class action lawsuits and shareholder rights litigation.

This press release may be considered Attorney Advertising in some jurisdictions under the applicable law and rules of ethics.             

CONTACT:

The Schall Law Firm
Brian Schall, Esq.,
www.schallfirm.com
Office: 310-301-3335
info@schallfirm.com

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/pins-investors-have-opportunity-to-lead-pinterest-inc-securities-fraud-lawsuit-with-the-schall-law-firm-302730831.html

SOURCE The Schall Law Firm

data.title: Evotec Appoints Dr. Ashiq H. Khan as Chief Commercial Officer
data.description:
HAMBURG, DE / ACCESS Newswire / April 1, 2026 / Evotec SE (NASDAQ:EVO)(Frankfurt Prime Standard:EVT) today announced the appointment of Dr. Ashiq H. Khan as EVP Global Head, Chief Commercial Officer. Dr. Khan brings to Evotec over 15 years of international leadership experience in biotech, CRO and AI-driven platform businesses, with a distinguished track record of managing diverse transcontinental teams and closing more than $7 billion in high-value standalone, integrated, and collaboration agreements across the U.S., EU and APAC markets. In his role, Dr. Khan will spearhead the build-out of a globally integrated, fit-for-purpose commercial organization fully aligned with Evotec's scientific leadership and platform capabilities to deliver enhanced customer value and drive more robust, sustainable growth.

Dr. Christian Wojczewski, Chief Executive Officer of Evotec, said:

"Ashiq has demonstrated tremendous success driving growth in innovative areas like robotics, artificial intelligence and cutting-edge drug discovery platforms. His expertise is essential for executing the next stage of Evotec's transformation as a responsive drug discovery and development engine built around high-value services and capabilities. We are excited to have someone with his experience in recognizing and creating new opportunities at the helm of our commercial team."

Dr. Khan most recently served as Chief Business Officer at Iktos. Prior to that, he held leadership roles at Proteros and Schrödinger. He holds a Ph.D. in Neurobiology from the University of Cologne and business degrees from the Wharton Business School and INSEAD.

Dr. Ashiq H. Khan, Evotec's designated Chief Commercial Officer, added:

"Evotec is uniquely positioned to advance early-stage drug discovery and development, underpinned by its industry-validated platforms, deep scientific expertise, and proven commercial models. As new technologies continue to transform the field, I am fully committed to working with Evotec's exceptional scientific, operational, and business leaders to further strengthen our capabilities and drive the next generation of breakthrough therapies."

About Evotec SE

Evotec is a life science company that is pioneering the future of drug discovery and development. By integrating breakthrough science with AI-driven innovation and advanced technologies, we accelerate the journey from concept to cure - faster, smarter, and with greater precision. Our expertise spans small molecules, biologics, cell therapies and associated modalities, supported by proprietary platforms such as Molecular Patient Databases, PanOmics and iPSC-based disease modeling. With flexible partnering models tailored to our customers' needs, we work with all Top 20 Pharma companies, over 800 biotechs, academic institutions, and healthcare stakeholders. Our offerings range from standalone services to fully integrated R&D programs and long-term strategic partnerships, combining scientific excellence with operational agility. Through Just - Evotec Biologics, we redefine biologics development and manufacturing to improve accessibility and affordability. With a strong portfolio of over 100 proprietary R&D assets, most of them being co-owned, we focus on key therapeutic areas including oncology, cardiovascular and metabolic diseases, neurology, and immunology. Evotec's global team of more than 4,800 experts operates from sites in Europe and the U.S., offering complementary technologies and services as synergistic centers of excellence. Learn more at www.evotec.com and follow us onLinkedIn and X/Twitter @Evotec.

Forward-looking statements

This announcement contains forward-looking statements concerning future events, including the proposed offering and listing of Evotec's securities. Words such as "anticipate," "believe," "could," "estimate," "expect," "intend," "may," "might," "plan," "potential," "should," "target," "would" and variations of such words and similar expressions are intended to identify forward-looking statements. Such statements include comments regarding Evotec's expectations for revenues, Group EBITDA and unpartnered R&D expenses. These forward-looking statements are based on the information available to, and the expectations and assumptions deemed reasonable by Evotec at the time these statements were made. No assurance can be given that such expectations will prove to have been correct. These statements involve known and unknown risks and are based upon a number of assumptions and estimates, which are inherently subject to significant uncertainties and contingencies, many of which are beyond the control of Evotec. Evotec expressly disclaims any obligations or undertaking to release publicly any updates or revisions to any forward-looking statements contained herein to reflect any change in Evotec's expectations with respect thereto or any change in events, conditions or circumstances on which any statement is based.

Investor Relations and Media Contact

Dr. Sarah Fakih
EVP Head of Global Communications & Investor Relations
Sarah.Fakih@evotec.com

SOURCE: Evotec SE

data.title: Indonesia Tightens Free-Float Rules in Bid to Boost Investor Confidence
data.description:
By Amanda Lee and Ying Xian Wong 




  Indonesia will give listed companies up to three years to meet a higher minimum free-float requirement in a bid to boost investor confidence. 



  The Indonesia Stock Exchange raised the minimum free float to 15% of total shares, from 7.5% previously, corporate secretary Kautsar Primadi Nurahmad said late Tuesday. 



  "These changes form part of Indonesia's broader efforts to accelerate capital market reform, enhance the quality of Listed Companies, strengthen corporate governance, and reinforce investor protection," he said. 



  The move comes months after index providers FTSE Russell and MSCI Inc. halted reviews of Indonesian securities over transparency concerns. 



  MSCI in January froze certain index-related changes for Indonesian securities, while FTSE Russell in February postponed a scheduled review of Indonesian stocks. 



  Under the new rules, companies must comply in stages based on market capitalization. 



  Firms with a market value below 5 trillion rupiah, equivalent to $295 million, must meet the requirement by March 31, 2029. Firms valued at 5 trillion rupiah or more must raise free float to 12.5% by March 31, 2027, and to 15% a year later. Those already  between 12.5% and 15% must reach 15% by March 31, 2027. 





  Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com and Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 01, 2026 01:38 ET (05:38 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Indonesia Inflation Eases in March as Base Effect Fades
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong 




  Indonesia's consumer inflation slowed in March, as a low base effect gradually faded. 



  Headline inflation was 3.48% in March, decelerating from 4.76% in February, as the impact of electricity tariff discounts in January and February last year waned,  the statistics agency said Wednesday. 



  Price pressures in March were mainly driven by higher utility costs. 



  Core inflation was 2.52%, compared with 2.63% in the previous month. Year-to-date inflation stood at 0.94%. 



  The conflict involving U.S. and Israel against Iran continues to unsettle global markets, fueling worries about an economic hit to Asian economies such as Indonesia that are vulnerable to commodity price disruptions. 



  Rising energy costs are increasing pressure on Indonesia to adjust fuel and electricity prices, though hikes to subsidized fuel are unlikely unless fiscal strains worsen, DBS senior economist Radhika Rao said in a recent note. 



  Higher fuel and fertilizer costs could push up inflation and food prices, she said. DBS raised its 2026 inflation forecast to 3.2% from 2.8% previously. 



  Given uncertainty and geopolitical risks, most economists think rate cuts are off the table for the Indonesian central bank. But given the supply-driven nature of the energy shock, Bank Indonesia is likely to resort to rate hikes if the currency weakens sharply or if fuel prices spiral for a sustained amount of time, analysts say. 



  DBS said its 2026 Indonesia growth forecast of 5.3% could also be revised if supply disruptions spill into the second and third quarters. 





  Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 01, 2026 01:30 ET (05:30 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

----

The Year Is Off to the Strongest Start for Big Deals Ever

Companies are plowing ahead with corporate tie-ups and investments despite a war and slumping stocks.

----

America Now Has an EV Rust Belt. High Gas Prices Won't Rescue It.

GM supplier Magna is stuck with a plant built to churn out parts for battery-powered pickups. "The magnitude of uncertainty is unparalleled," said its CEO.

----

Fed Should Be Ready to Act to Address Inflation Concerns, Kansas City Fed's Schmid Says

The Fed president said the central bank should be prepared to address elevated inflation proactively so that it doesn't get stuck near 3% in the long run.

----

Gas Just Hit $4 a Gallon. Is That Really as Bad as It Sounds?

Sure, gasoline has cost more in the past. But dealing with a rapid run-up in prices is a lot harder than dealing with a gradual one.

----

U.S. Home Price Growth Slowed in January

U.S. home-price growth slowed in January as affordability constraints continued to weigh on home buyer decisions.

----

U.S. Job Openings and Hiring Fell in February

Available positions fell to 6.9 million from an upwardly revised 7.2 million in January, and hiring fell to its lowest level since April 2020.

----

Consumers' Mood Steady Amid Iran War, Conference Board Survey Suggests

The Conference Board's sentiment index rose to 91.8, from 91 in February. Analysts polled by The Wall Street Journal were anticipating a March decline to 87.5.

----

Canada Economy Accelerates After GDP Grows in January

Economic activity in Canada remained positive in the early months of the year despite volatility in manufacturing and continued unease over trade.

----

European Banks Approach End of Three-Year Bull Run

The Stoxx 600 Europe Banks index was down around 7% for the year, threatening to turn the tide on a three-year run. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 01, 2026 01:15 ET (05:15 GMT)

data.title: Reju Secures €135 Million in Dutch NIKI Funding for Industrial-Scale Textile-to-Textile Regeneration Hub at Chemelot Industrial Park, the Netherlands
data.description:
Reju Secures €135 Million in Dutch NIKI Funding for Industrial-Scale Textile-to-Textile Regeneration Hub at Chemelot Industrial Park, the Netherlands

Reju Secures €135 Million in Dutch NIKI Funding for Industrial-Scale Textile-to-Textile Regeneration Hub at Chemelot Industrial Park, the Netherlands

PR Newswire

PARIS, April 1, 2026

PARIS, April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Reju™, the progressive textile-to-textile regeneration company, has been awarded €135 million in funding under the Netherlands' Nationale Investeringsregeling Klimaatprojecten Industrie (NIKI) program. The funding will support Reju's planned industrial-scale Regeneration Hub at Chemelot Industrial Park in Sittard-Geleen, covering both the investment phase and ongoing operations, and represents a critical milestone on the path toward final investment decision.

"We are grateful to the Government of the Netherlands and the Ministry of Economic Affairs and Climate for supporting the scale-up of commercial technologies that can deliver measurable emissions reductions and accelerate the transition to a truly circular textile industry," said Patrik Frisk, CEO of Reju. "This award is a strong vote of confidence in our technology and our team. At Chemelot, we will deliver circular raw materials at scale, reduce emissions across textile value chains, and establish a replicable blueprint for circular textiles in Europe."

NIKI is the Dutch government's flagship program to accelerate large-scale industrial decarbonization and circularity, supporting both national and European Union circular economy objectives. Reju's project is closely aligned with these goals, expanding a textile-to-textile process that converts difficult-to-recycle, polyester-containing textiles into high-quality circular intermediates for new polyester production. By diverting residual textile fractions from landfill and incineration, Reju, aims to materially reduce the environmental impact of textile waste.

The future Regeneration Hub will process post-consumer textiles that would otherwise enter the waste stream. This regenerated output will be transformed into Reju Polyester, delivering approximately 50% lower carbon emissions compared with virgin polyester. The material will then be reintroduced into downstream supply chains, where it will be converted into yarns and fabrics ready for end-use consumer applications.

The project is expected to emphasize industrial integration, energy and resource efficiency, and fully traceable circular supply chains, maximizing the displacement of virgin, fossil-based inputs.

Chemelot Industrial Park was selected for its established industrial ecosystem, shared utilities and logistics infrastructure, and proximity to innovation and research capabilities. These attributes are expected to support efficient ramp-up, operational reliability, and the replication of the model across future sites.

About Reju

Reju is a materials regeneration company focused on creating innovative solutions for regenerating polyester textiles and post-consumer PET waste. Owned by Technip Energies and utilizing technology originating with IBM Research, Reju is driven by its purpose to unlock infinite possibilities within finite resources. The company aims to establish a textile circular system to regenerate and recirculate polyester textiles. Learn more at www.reju.com.

About Technip Energies

Technip Energies is a global technology and engineering powerhouse. With leadership positions in LNG, hydrogen, ethylene, sustainable chemistry, and CO2 management, we are contributing to the development of critical markets such as energy, energy derivatives, decarbonization, and circularity. Our complementary business segments, Technology, Products and Services (TPS) and Project Delivery, turn innovation into scalable and industrial reality.

Through collaboration and excellence in execution, our 18,000+ employees across 35 countries are fully committed to bridging prosperity with sustainability for a world designed to last.

Technip Energies generated revenues of €7.2 billion in 2025 and is listed on Euronext Paris. The Company also has American Depositary Receipts trading over the counter. For further information: www.ten.com

Media Contact

Chris Goddard, CGPR, chris@cgprpublicrelations.com
Tali Serantes-James, Reju, tali.serantes@reju.com

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/reju-secures-135-million-in-dutch-niki-funding-for-industrial-scale-textile-to-textile-regeneration-hub-at-chemelot-industrial-park-the-netherlands-302730558.html

SOURCE Reju

data.title: Asian Equities, Government Bonds Rise on Hopes for Quick End to Mideast Conflict — Update
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui and Sherry Qin 




  Asian equities and government bonds rose on Wednesday as hopes for a quick end to the Middle East conflict soothed concerns over elevated inflationary pressures driven by higher-for-longer oil prices. 



  President Trump told reporters in the Oval Office on Tuesday, "We'll be leaving very soon," saying the U.S. would leave Iran in two to three weeks. Trump plans to deliver a speech on Wednesday evening to provide "an important update on Iran," according to White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt. Meanwhile, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said Tehran is ready to end the conflict but wants unspecified guarantees. 



  "More credible steps toward de-escalation are finally emerging, critically including on Iran's side," five members of Westpac Strategy Group said in commentary. "At the very least, markets have received a welcome jolt that is forcing a repricing in the distribution of outcomes" from the conflict, the members added. 



  South Korea's Kospi ended its losing streak and jumped 7.1%, Japan's Nikkei Stock Average rose 4.3%. Taiwan's Taiex Index added 4.4%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index was 2.0% higher. 



  Risk sentiment spurred gains in chip stocks, which have been tied to the AI boom. Samsung Electronics rose 12% and SK Hynix gained 9.05%. Kioxia Holdings jumped 12%, and TSMC added 4.8%. 



  Energy shipping stocks also advanced, with Cosco Shipping Energy Transportation rising 10% in Hong Kong and China Merchants Energy Shipping gaining 10% in Shanghai. 



  Yields on 10-year Japanese government bonds fell 4.5bps to 2.310%, those on 10-year Australian sovereign securities declined 6bps to 4.9150%, and those on 10-year New Zealand government debt dropped 12bps to 4.6100%. 



  "The mood in markets was fairly jubilant" after Trump's remarks, said Capital Economics' Thomas Mathews. However, he warns that higher energy prices would remain an economic drag and keep major equity indices from immediately returning to their previous highs. 



  However, oil prices remained elevated after Trump told aides that he is willing to end the U.S. military campaign against Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz stays mostly closed, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing administration officials. Trump also urged other countries to launch their own operations to wrest control of the strait from Iran. The waterway handled around a fifth of global energy flows before the conflict. 



  Trump's suggestion that other countries could reopen the Strait of Hormuz is a "reminder that supply risks remain firmly in play," StoneX's Matt Simpson said in commentary. "If the U.S. were to step back without restoring flows through [the Strait of] Hormuz, the most likely outcome is persistently higher oil prices and renewed inflation pressures--a scenario markets may not be fully pricing," the senior market analyst added. 



  Front-month West Texas Intermediate crude oil futures rose 1.5% to $102.85 per barrel, and front-month Brent crude oil futures gained 1.2% to $105.19 a barrel, ICE data showed. 



  Asian currencies were mixed against the dollar. The U.S. dollar was 0.3% higher at 1,511.52 Korean won, but 0.3% lower at 60.39 Philippine pesos. 





  Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com and Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 01, 2026 00:56 ET (04:56 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
